validation(3) Library Functions Manual validation(3)

NAME

validation - Validation Helpers


- Reusable validation macros for protocol handlers.

SYNOPSIS

Topics


Image Validation Functions

Files


file validation.h
Common validation macros to reduce duplication in protocol handlers.

Macros


#define VALIDATE_NOTNULL_DATA(client, data, packet_name)
#define VALIDATE_MIN_SIZE(client, len, min_size, packet_name)
#define VALIDATE_EXACT_SIZE(client, len, expected_size, packet_name)
#define VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED(client, packet_name)
#define VALIDATE_AUDIO_SAMPLE_COUNT(client, num_samples, max_samples, packet_name)
#define VALIDATE_AUDIO_ALIGNMENT(client, len, sample_size, packet_name)
#define VALIDATE_RESOURCE_INITIALIZED(client, resource, resource_name)
#define VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE(client, data, len, expected_size, packet_name)
#define VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, value, value_name, packet_name)
#define VALIDATE_RANGE(client, value, min_val, max_val, value_name, packet_name)
#define VALIDATE_CAPABILITY_FLAGS(client, flags, valid_mask, packet_name)
#define VALIDATE_FLAGS_MASK(client, flags, valid_mask, packet_name)
#define VALIDATE_PACKET_NOT_NULL(client, data, packet_name)

Typedefs


typedef struct client_info client_info_t
Per-client state structure for server-side client management.

Functions


void disconnect_client_for_bad_data (client_info_t *client, const char *format,...)

Detailed Description

Reusable validation macros for protocol handlers.

Provides standardized validation macros for protocol handlers to:

  • Check for NULL pointers
  • Validate payload sizes
  • Verify client state flags
  • Reduce code duplication across handlers

All macros call disconnect_client_for_bad_data() on validation failure and return from the calling function immediately.

Usage:

void handle_packet(client_info_t *client, const void *data, size_t len) {


  VALIDATE_NOTNULL_DATA(client, data, "PACKET_TYPE");


  VALIDATE_MIN_SIZE(client, len, 16, "PACKET_TYPE");


  VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED(client, "PACKET_TYPE");


  // ... continue processing ...
}

Macro Definition Documentation

#define VALIDATE_AUDIO_ALIGNMENT( client, len, sample_size, packet_name)

#include <include/ascii-chat/util/validation.h>Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  if ((len) % (sample_size) != 0) {                                                                                  \


    disconnect_client_for_bad_data((client), "%s payload not aligned (len=%zu, sample_size=%zu)", (packet_name),     \


                                   (len), (sample_size));                                                            \


    return;                                                                                                          \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)


Validate that audio sample alignment is correct (must be multiple
    of sample size). Disconnects client and returns if alignment is
  incorrect.


Definition at line 111 of file
  util/validation.h.


111 #define VALIDATE_AUDIO_ALIGNMENT(client, len, sample_size, packet_name)                                                \
112   do {                                                                                                                 \
113     if ((len) % (sample_size) != 0) {                                                                                  \
114       disconnect_client_for_bad_data((client), "%s payload not aligned (len=%zu, sample_size=%zu)", (packet_name),     \
115                                      (len), (sample_size));                                                            \
116       return;                                                                                                          \
117     }                                                                                                                  \
118   } while (0)






Referenced by handle_audio_packet().






#define
  VALIDATE_AUDIO_SAMPLE_COUNT( client, num_samples, max_samples,
  packet_name)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/util/validation.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  if ((num_samples) <= 0 || (num_samples) > (max_samples)) {                                                         \


    disconnect_client_for_bad_data((client), "%s invalid sample count: %d (max %d)", (packet_name), (num_samples),   \


                                   (max_samples));                                                                   \


    return;                                                                                                          \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Validate that audio sample count is within acceptable bounds.
    Disconnects client and returns if sample count is invalid.

#define
  VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED( client, packet_name)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/util/validation.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  if (!atomic_load_bool(&(client)->is_sending_audio)) {                                                                   \


    disconnect_client_for_bad_data((client), "%s received before audio stream enabled", (packet_name));              \


    return;                                                                                                          \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Validate that audio stream is enabled for this client. Disconnects
    client and returns if audio stream is not enabled.

#define
  VALIDATE_CAPABILITY_FLAGS( client, flags, valid_mask, packet_name)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/util/validation.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  if (((flags) & (valid_mask)) == 0) {                                                                               \


    disconnect_client_for_bad_data((client), "%s no valid capability flags set (flags=0x%x, valid=0x%x)",            \


                                   (packet_name), (unsigned)(flags), (unsigned)(valid_mask));                        \


    return;                                                                                                          \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Validate that at least one capability flag is set. Disconnects
    client and returns if all flags are zero or invalid.

  util/validation.h.

#define
  VALIDATE_EXACT_SIZE( client, len, expected_size, packet_name)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/util/validation.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  if ((len) != (expected_size)) {                                                                                    \


    disconnect_client_for_bad_data((client), "%s payload size mismatch (len=%zu, expected=%zu)", (packet_name),      \


                                   (len), (expected_size));                                                          \


    return;                                                                                                          \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Validate that payload size is exactly the expected size.
    Disconnects client and returns if len != expected_size.

#define
  VALIDATE_FLAGS_MASK( client, flags, valid_mask, packet_name)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/util/validation.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  if (((flags) & ~(valid_mask)) != 0) {                                                                              \


    disconnect_client_for_bad_data((client), "%s unknown flags set (flags=0x%x, valid=0x%x)", (packet_name),         \


                                   (unsigned)(flags), (unsigned)(valid_mask));                                       \


    return;                                                                                                          \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Validate that only known/valid flags are set. Disconnects client
    and returns if unknown flags are present.

  if ((value) == 0) {                                                                                                \

    etc). Disconnects client and returns if value is zero.

#define
  VALIDATE_PACKET_NOT_NULL( client, data, packet_name)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/util/validation.h>Value:


({                                                                                                                   \


  int _validation_failed = 0;                                                                                        \


  if (!(data)) {                                                                                                     \


    disconnect_client_for_bad_data((client), packet_name " payload missing");                                        \


    _validation_failed = 1;                                                                                          \


  }                                                                                                                  \


  _validation_failed;                                                                                                \
})




Validate packet payload pointer is not NULL. Returns true if
    validation failed (data is NULL), false if valid.

  VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE( client, data, len, expected_size, packet_name)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/util/validation.h>Value:

  if (!(data)) {                                                                                                     \


    disconnect_client_for_bad_data((client), packet_name " payload missing");                                        \

    is non-NULL and payload matches expected size.

#define
  VALIDATE_RANGE( client, value, min_val, max_val, value_name,
  packet_name)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/util/validation.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  if ((value) < (min_val) || (value) > (max_val)) {                                                                  \


    disconnect_client_for_bad_data((client), "%s %s out of range: %u (valid: %u-%u)", (packet_name), (value_name),   \


                                   (unsigned)(value), (unsigned)(min_val), (unsigned)(max_val));                     \


    return;                                                                                                          \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Validate that a numeric value is within a specified range
    (inclusive). Disconnects client and returns if value is outside range.

  util/validation.h.

    Disconnects client and returns if resource is NULL.

Typedef
  Documentation




typedef
  struct client_info client_info_t


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/network/client.h>


Per-client state structure for server-side client management.
    Represents complete state for a single connected client in the ascii-chat
    server. This structure stores all client-specific information including
    network connection, media capabilities, terminal settings, threading state,
    and synchronization primitives.










CORE
  FIELDS: 


    
  
  • Network: Socket, IP address, port, thread handles
    • 
  
  • Media: Video/audio capabilities, stream state, buffers
    • 
  
  • Terminal: Capabilities, palette cache, dimensions
    • 
  
  • Threading: Send/receive/render thread handles and flags
    • 
  
  • Synchronization: Mutexes for thread-safe state access
    • 
  
  • Crypto: Cryptographic handshake context for secure communication
    • 







THREADING
  MODEL: 


Each client has dedicated threads:


    
  
  • receive_thread: Handles incoming packets (protocol processing)
    • 
  
  • send_thread: Manages outgoing packet delivery (packet queues)
    • 
  
  • video_render_thread: Generates ASCII frames at 60fps
    • 
  
  • audio_render_thread: Mixes audio streams at 172fps
    • 







BUFFER
  MANAGEMENT: 


    
  
  • incoming_video_buffer: Double-buffered video frames from client
    • 
  
  • incoming_audio_buffer: Ring buffer for client's audio samples
    • 
  
  • outgoing_video_buffer: Double-buffered ASCII frames to send
    • 
  
  • audio_queue: Packet queue for audio packets to send
    • 







MEMORY
  MANAGEMENT: 


    
  
  • Pre-allocated buffers to avoid malloc/free in hot paths
    • 
  
  • send_buffer: For packet assembly
    • 
  
  • crypto_plaintext_buffer: For encryption plaintext
    • 
  
  • crypto_ciphertext_buffer: For encryption ciphertext
    • 



Note


All atomic fields are thread-safe for concurrent access.



Buffer pointers are set once during client creation and never
    change.




Thread handles are valid only when threads are running.








Function
  Documentation




void
  disconnect_client_for_bad_data (client_info_t * client, const char *
  format, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/util/validation.h>


Definition at line 154 of file
  server/protocol.c.


154                                                                                     {
155   if (!client) {
156     return;
157   }
158 
159   protocol_cleanup_thread_locals();
160 
161   // Atomically set the disconnect flag and get the old value
162   // This ensures only one thread proceeds to disconnect (the first one)
163   bool already_requested = atomic_exchange_bool(&client->protocol_disconnect_requested, true);
164   if (already_requested) {
165     return;  // Already disconnecting, don't do it again
166   }
167 
168   char reason[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL] = {0};
169   if (format) {
170     va_list args;
171     va_start(args, format);
172     safe_vsnprintf(reason, sizeof(reason), format, args);
173     va_end(args);
174   } else {
175     SAFE_STRNCPY(reason, "Protocol violation", sizeof(reason));
176   }
177 
178   const char *reason_str = reason[0] != '\0' ? reason : "Protocol violation";
179   const char *client_id = client->client_id;
180 
181   socket_t socket_snapshot = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
182   const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = NULL;
183   acip_transport_t *transport_snapshot = NULL;
184 
185   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
186   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
187     socket_snapshot = client->socket;
188     if (client->crypto_initialized) {
189       crypto_ctx = crypto_handshake_get_context(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
190     }
191   }
192   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
193 
194   // Get transport reference for WebSocket clients
195   mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
196   transport_snapshot = client->transport;
197   mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
198 
199   // NOTE: Disconnecting a client due to the client's own bad behavior isn't an
200   // error for us, it's desired behavior for us, so we simply warn and do not
201   // have a need for asciichat_errno here.
202   log_warn("Disconnecting client %u due to protocol violation: %s", client_id, reason_str);
203 
204   if (socket_snapshot != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
205     // Protect socket writes with send_mutex to prevent race with send_thread.
206     // This receive_thread and send_thread both write to same socket.
207     mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
208 
209     asciichat_error_t log_result =
210         log_network_message(socket_snapshot, (const struct crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx, LOG_ERROR,
211                             REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_SERVER_TO_CLIENT, "Protocol violation: %s", reason_str);
212     if (log_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
213       log_warn("Failed to send remote log to client %u: %s", client_id, asciichat_error_string(log_result));
214     }
215 
216     asciichat_error_t send_result = packet_send_error(socket_snapshot, crypto_ctx, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, reason_str);
217     if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
218       log_warn("Failed to send error packet to client %u: %s", client_id, asciichat_error_string(send_result));
219     }
220 
221     mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
222   } else if (transport_snapshot) {
223     // For WebSocket clients, try to send error via transport
224     log_debug("Sending error to WebSocket client %u via transport", client_id);
225     acip_send_error(transport_snapshot, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, reason_str);
226   }
227 
228   platform_sleep_ms(500);
229 
230   log_debug("Setting active=false in disconnect_client_for_bad_data (client_id=%u, reason=%s)", client_id, reason_str);
231   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
232   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, true);
233   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
234   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
235   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
236 
237   if (client->audio_queue) {
238     packet_queue_stop(client->audio_queue);
239   }
240 
241   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
242   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
243     socket_shutdown(client->socket, 2);
244     socket_close(client->socket);
245     client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
246   }
247   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
248 }






References acip_send_error(), client_info::active,
    args, ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_exchange_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), client_info::audio_queue,
    client_info::audio_render_thread_running, BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL,
    client_info::client_id, client_info::client_state_mutex,
    client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_get_context(),
    client_info::crypto_initialized, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, log_debug, LOG_ERROR,
    log_network_message(), log_warn, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, packet_queue_stop(), packet_send_error(),
    platform_sleep_ms(),
    client_info::protocol_disconnect_requested,
    REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_SERVER_TO_CLIENT, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    safe_vsnprintf(), client_info::send_mutex,
    client_info::send_thread_running, client_info::shutting_down,
    client_info::socket, socket_close(), socket_shutdown(),
    client_info::transport, and
    client_info::video_render_thread_running.


Referenced by handle_audio_batch_packet(),
    handle_audio_opus_batch_packet(), handle_audio_opus_packet(),
    handle_client_join_packet(), handle_image_frame_h265_packet(),
    handle_image_frame_packet(), handle_protocol_version_packet(),
    handle_remote_log_packet_from_client(), and
    process_decrypted_packet().
